title: Euphrasia sennenii
taxonomy: Euphrasia sennenii is a species of plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. The genus Euphrasia is a group of annual or perennial herbaceous plants, commonly referred to as eyebrights. These plants are native to the Mediterranean region and are widely distributed in Asia and Europe. Euphrasia sennenii is specifically known to be endemic to China. Its classification is further supported by its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system.

intro: Euphrasia sennenii, also known as Sennen's eyebright, is a flowering plant species within the genus Euphrasia, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and slender leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat eye and upper respiratory tract infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Euphrasia sennenii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Euphrasia sennenii is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or eye wash, and is used to treat conditions such as conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and sinusitis.
